title: narcissus tazetta subsp. bertolonii
intro: Narcissus tazetta subsp. bertolonii, also known as the Bertoloni's daffodil, is a subspecies of the tazetta narcissus, a perennial plant that belongs to the Amaryllidaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Narcissus tazetta subsp. bertolonii include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
